body:
TUNGSTEN 9.43 A7.5 QUANT SLS(23) delivered -- two-layer dense hardening pass complete on SLS(22), compiles 0 errors/1 pre-existing warning. Files in files/from-agent.

ARE WE CLOSER TO A COMPLETE, TRUSTWORTHY PRODUCT? Yes, meaningfully -- but not fully there, and here is exactly why both halves are true.

WHY CLOSER: this pass found and fixed real silent-failure bugs, not cosmetic ones. Two independent freeze bugs: (1) the OnTick re-entrancy guard leaked permanently true on the drawdown-blacklist exit path -- one bad regime/session and the EA silently stopped trading AND stopped managing already-open positions forever, while still looking alive in the logs; (2) a pattern-database lock was incremented once and never released anywhere in the 54k-line file -- from the second closed trade onward every trade close froze the whole MT4 terminal thread for up to 5 seconds. Also fixed: a return-vs-continue bug that silently skipped breakeven protection on other open orders; a live risk-percent clamp swallowed into a malformed comment (unbounded sizing); four separate ring-buffer reads using fixed indices instead of proper wraparound (drawdown response, recal-recovery, Slingshot, scale-in were all silently grading themselves on stale trades); and -- the structurally biggest one -- the probability/conviction database was blind to short trades entirely (bucket range only covered positive scores) while simultaneously corrupting its long-side data with misfiled short-trade outcomes. Half the strategy's directional space had zero statistical protection until this fix. Also found GenerateIntelligentSignal computing three separate confidence/threshold adjustments (regime-confidence trim, CEO-alignment multiplier, candle-anatomy cross-validation) that were then unconditionally discarded before ever affecting a real decision -- dead work masquerading as active safeguards. None of these threw errors or crashed anything, which is exactly why they're dangerous for a system meant to run unattended and be trusted.

WHY NOT FULLY COMPLETE: two real gaps remain. (1) S4 -- the fresh calibration + ghost validation run -- has never completed successfully on any SLS22/23 build. It was blocked first on manual MT4 attach, then the market closed for the weekend right after attach with only Phase 0 confirmed passing. So there are still no real WR/PF/ghost-verdict numbers for this exact hardened codebase -- everything above is a static-analysis correctness pass, not a validated backtest. (2) The account hit a session-usage rate limit twice mid-review, so Layer 2's second independent read only got a fresh full pass on 2 of 8 code sections (both found and fixed real bugs) -- the rest were verified narrowly (re-checking the exact lines Layer 1 touched, resolving two reachability questions) rather than re-read blind end to end. That's a real, acknowledged residual risk, not a hidden one.

NEXT STEP: drag TUNGSTEN_9.43_A7.5_QUANT_SLS(23).ex4 onto the USDJPY M5 chart to replace the SLS(22) instance (MT4 doesn't hot-swap), then let S4 actually complete once the market's open, so we finally get real numbers instead of just "it compiles and the logic reads correctly."
